item.title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
item.description: BBC News understands the yacht had drifted towards the Admiral Grigorovich, a Russian frigate which has been operating in the Channel.

item.title: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
item.description: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

item.title: Fragile quiet in Lebanon as US-Iran truce leaves unanswered questions
item.description: Many Lebanese remain doubtful that the agreement could finally mean the end of the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.

item.title: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
item.description: Robert Kuzovkov, who used the pseudonym Semyon Skrepetsky, has been known for his caricatures of politicians including Vladimir Putin.

item.title: France's oldest female detainee, 79, goes on trial for in-law's grisly murder
item.description: A dismembered body found in a chain-bound trunk in the Seine in 1995 was only recently connected to the defendant by DNA evidence.

item.title: Venezuela signs deal with US energy giant to rebuild power grid
item.description: The deal with General Electric is the latest sign of co-operation between US firms and Venezuela's interim government.

item.title: Eight dead after US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes in California
item.description: The incident occurred on Monday morning while the aircraft had been on a routine test mission.

item.title: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
item.description: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

item.title: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
item.description: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

item.title: 'Dancing girl's' bare torso restored in Indian textbook after backlash
item.description: A picture in a new school textbook had covered up the naked torso of the famous figurine with dark shading.

item.title: Five big questions about the UK's under-16s social media ban
item.description: A ban is coming - but it's still not clear what it will mean for sites including Roblox, YouTube and WhatsApp.

item.title: When will social media ban start, and which apps will be affected?
item.description: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.

item.title: What is Helium-3 and could we get it from the moon?
item.description: Helium-3 is expensive and demand is forecast to soar, so some are planning to mine it on the moon.

item.title: Social media ban - bold and blunt, but no silver bullet
item.description: The BBC's technology editor Zoe Kleinman on the big changes coming down the line for young people online.

item.title: Under-16s will be banned from social media from early 2027
item.description: Sites including Instagram, YouTube and TikTok will become inaccessible for millions of children, the prime minister has announced.

item.title: Elon Musk's stratospheric rise to trillionaire status - in charts
item.description: The BBC breaks down how the tech mogul's fortune has grown.

item.title: Who is Elon Musk and what is his net worth?
item.description: The boss of X, Tesla and SpaceX, already the world's richest person, is now also its first trillionaire.

item.title: Social media on trial: Four important cases to watch
item.description: Social media firms face thousands of lawsuits, the BBC looks at four which could be significant.

item.title: Kalshi to make some users reveal job details to tackle insider trading
item.description: After issues with insider trading, the prediction betting platform is adding new rules.

item.title: Version of AI tool 'too powerful for public' released to public
item.description: Claude Fable 5 is a version of Anthropic's Claude Mythos, an AI program which caused a stir among technology, finance, and government leaders.

item.title: Musk's SpaceX overtakes Amazon to become world's fifth most valuable firm
item.description: Elon Musk's rocket firm has overtaken the retail and media empire in value after a surge in its share price.

item.title: Struggling Pizza Hut chain to be sold for $2.7bn
item.description: The decision comes after a prolonged period of difficulty for the chain, which has faced increasing competition from a range of rivals.

item.title: Thames Water moves step closer to nationalisation after government objects to rescue deal
item.description: The environment secretary says the deal does not do enough for consumers or the environment.

item.title: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
item.description: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

item.title: How the Iran war affects your money and bills
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has increased pressure on the cost of petrol, household energy bills and even food.

item.title: Why is Thames Water in so much trouble?
item.description: Thames Water's lenders have submitted a new rescue plan to save the firm, but how did it end up in this state?

item.title: How could the US-Iran deal affect oil prices and the cost of food?
item.description: Experts warn the impact of the war will continue to affect the global economy for months to come.

item.title: What's happening to UK petrol and diesel prices now the US and Iran have a deal?
item.description: When the conflict began on 28 February, fuel costs jumped as the war disrupted the production and transportation of energy across the Middle East.

item.title: Is the convertible heading into the sunset?
item.description: UK drivers have taken a shine to the SUV but could the fate of the convertible be reversed?

item.title: What is El Niño and why could it mean record temperatures?
item.description: Global temperatures are expected to rise in the coming months as El Niño begins.

item.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
item.description: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

item.title: Six ways to keep your home and yourself cool in hot weather
item.description: Six simple things you can do to help keep your house cool when temperatures rise.

item.title: Nasa names next astronauts for Artemis Moon programme
item.description: Nasa names its next Artemis crew, though they will not be walking on the Moon or even going anywhere near it.

item.title: Nasa has named the Artemis III crew - what is their mission?
item.description: Artemis III will help test crucial systems needed for the planned Artemis IV Moon landing in 2028.

item.title: Rare footage captured of Great White shark in Mediterranean Sea
item.description: A volunteer diver has described shaking as he filmed his encounter with an endangered Great White shark between Tunisia and Sicily.

item.title: Astronauts return to ISS after sheltering during air leak repair attempt
item.description: Russian attempt to repair tunnel area sparks safe-haven procedure for five other astronauts onboard.

item.title: Mangrove forests are healing after decades of human destruction
item.description: Swampy mangrove forests are staging a surprise comeback - which is good news for coastal communities and the climate.

item.title: Failing sea defences 'disaster' for nature reserve
item.description: An internationally important nature reserve in Hampshire is under threat from failing flood defences.

item.title: Next El Niño could be strongest in decades
item.description: A new phase of the natural El Niño weather pattern could begin in a matter of weeks, the UN has warned, boosting temperatures on a planet already under strain from climate change.

item.title: Why you might not be buying the right pain relief for period cramps
item.description: Many women are buying less effective pain medication for period cramps, supermarket data suggests.

item.title: Resident doctors cancel strike after new offer from government
item.description: The walkout had been due to start at 07:00 BST on Monday and last until Friday.

item.title: 'I've never been this good' – revolutionary immune reset puts lupus in remission
item.description: Patients on the trial have not needed medication to manage their condition.

item.title: 'I spent uni savings on getting my teeth fixed' - the real cost of NHS dentist shortage
item.description: People tell BBC Your Voice the rising cost of private dentistry is putting them in a difficult position.

item.title: Meningitis B vaccine to be offered to a million young people
item.description: The decision for the one-off vaccine programme follows the unprecedented outbreak in Kent this year.

item.title: Weight-loss drug Wegovy to be available in pill form in UK for first time
item.description: Manufacturer Novo Nordisk says a daily tablet of the drug could be more convenient for some people than weekly injections.

item.title: Nearly 3,000 patients a day face corridor care in NHS
item.description: New data reveals sheer scale of patients in England being treated in unsafe and undignified make-shift areas.

item.title: Advanced radiotherapy for prostate cancer to cut sessions from 20 to five
item.description: Some men in England with the disease will now be offered an advanced form of treatment on the NHS.

item.title: How Eriksen's heart device kicked into action
item.description: Denmark's team doctor said an ICD implanted into the footballer's chest responded as it should after he collapsed on Sunday.

item.title: New drug to stop 'Ozempic butt' muscle loss side effect of obesity jabs
item.description: A third of the weight loss from obesity jabs can come from muscle, say experts.

item.title: UK forces face operational cuts without more cash, defence chief warns
item.description: It comes as ex-defence secretary John Healey says the UK's enemies "do not follow timetables set by the Treasury".

item.title: Russian warship fires warning shots near UK-registered yacht in Channel
item.description: BBC News understands the yacht had drifted towards the Admiral Grigorovich, a Russian frigate which has been operating in the Channel.

item.title: Streeting warns against 'expensive' Labour leadership pledges
item.description: The ex-health secretary says he would not make campaign promises that he would have to later reverse.

item.title: UK will play full part in reopening Strait of Hormuz, Starmer says
item.description: The prime minister says the UK is working with other countries on a plan to protect vessels in the key waterway.

item.title: Labour activist admits vote rigging offence
item.description: Gabriel Leroy, a former councillor in Southend, pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit an offence under the Criminal Law Act and the Computer Misuse Act.

item.title: Row over potential cull of Dartmoor hill ponies
item.description: Campaigners say requirements to cut livestock grazing could lead to a 90% cull of its hill ponies.

item.title: Starmer pleased 'justice has been done' after arson attacks
item.description: The prime minister said he was "very pleased for my family's sake" that two men had been convicted of the 2025 arson attacks.

item.title: Labour risks handing power to Reform without drastic change, says union leader
item.description: Andrea Egan tellls the BBC that Labour haven't delivered and her election demonstrated that members were desperate to have their voices heard.

item.title: Sister of Jo Cox urges 'common ground' 10 years after her murder
item.description: Spen Valley MP Kim Leadbeater reflects a decade after her sister was murdered in Birstall.
